pattern: A bitmask-over-remainder feasibility table tells you which partial arrangements can still reach a divisible whole, letting a greedy pass commit the smallest next number that keeps a solution alive.
hints: Concatenating a number shifts the running value by its digit count, so the only state that matters is which numbers are used and the current remainder modulo k.
Precompute, for every (used-set, remainder) pair, whether the leftover numbers can be ordered to finish at remainder zero.

Once feasibility is known, build the answer greedily: at each slot try the unused numbers from smallest value upward and take the first one whose resulting (set, remainder) state can still be completed; if even the empty set at remainder zero is infeasible, return an empty list.

edge_inputs.description: swap needed for divisibility by ten
k of one accepts any order, lex smallest wins

stress.description: several multi-digit numbers with a modest k so a solution usually exists
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(1, 9)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(1, 30)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: small n keeps the 2^n by k table fast
